subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Nancy Roman: Mother of Hubble
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nancy Roman: Mother of Hubble.

hook.text: Nancy Roman was NASA's first Chief of Astronomy. She championed the space telescope that became Hubble.

buildup.text: In the 1960s, she argued that a telescope above Earth's atmosphere would revolutionize astronomy. NASA listened.

discovery.text: She oversaw the planning and advocacy that made the Hubble Space Telescope possible, fighting for funding for decades.

twist.text: When Hubble launched in 1990, its mirror was flawed. Astronauts fixed it in 1993—and it became the most famous telescope ever.

climax.text: Hubble has produced over 1.5 million observations, revealing the age, size, and expansion rate of the universe.

punchline.text: She fought for a telescope in space. It changed everything we know.

quiz.question: Why is Nancy Roman called the 'Mother of Hubble'?
options: She championed and oversaw the planning of the Hubble Space Telescope at NASA
She was the first person to observe through the Hubble telescope
She designed the mirror that was installed in Hubble
